subject: English
chapter_name: Verghese Kurien—I Too Had A Dream
overall_learning_objectives: Analyse the leadership qualities and vision of Verghese Kurien
Evaluate the social and economic impact of the White Revolution
Understand the role of cooperative movements in rural development
Develop critical thinking through debate and research activities
Compare Kurien’s achievements with other national leaders
Enhance reading comprehension, analytical writing, and vocabulary skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Social Change and Visionary Leaders
topics_covered: Role of visionary leaders in development
student_activities: List qualities of a visionary leader
Discuss how one person’s idea can transform society

lesson_plan.title: Biographical Study of Verghese Kurien
topics_covered: Beginning of the dairy cooperative movement
student_activities: Create a detailed timeline of Kurien’s life
Identify challenges faced during his early career

lesson_plan.title: The White Revolution and Dairy Cooperatives
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation with charts and diagrams
student_activities: Analyse how cooperative societies help farmers
Write key points explaining the White Revolution

lesson_plan.title: Critical Analysis of Leadership
student_activities: Analyse how Kurien’s decisions helped farmers
Compare Kurien’s leadership with another national leader

lesson_plan.title: Impact and Debate Activity
topics_covered: Economic and social impact of the White Revolution
Role of cooperatives in modern agriculture
student_activities: Debate: 'Cooperative systems are better than private systems for farmers'

lesson_plan.title: Language Development and Research Presentation
topics_covered: Research summary of the White Revolution
student_activities: Use new vocabulary words in sentences
Present a short report on the White Revolution

teaching_aids: Map of India showing dairy production regions
Images or videos of AMUL and dairy farming
teacher_tip: Encourage students to research real examples of dairy cooperatives like AMUL and present how these organisations improved farmers’ livelihoods. This helps students connect the chapter to real-world economic development.
